jokes.text: Now, that sign says 'applesauce.' No, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'
jokes.setup: Audience warm-up comedian points to applause sign
jokes.punchline: Deliberate mispronunciation followed by correction
jokes.explanation: Basic warm-up comedy - functional but predictable wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Warm-up Comedian
dialogue.line: Now, that sign says 'applesauce.' No, I'm kidding. It says 'applause.'

jokes.text: And now, because it's an F.A.A. Regulation... your flight attendant, Karen Jackson.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous premise that talk show needs flight attendant by law
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist premise that sets up extended airline parody - good economy and originality

dialogue.line: And now, because it's an F.A.A. Regulation... your flight attendant, Karen Jackson.

jokes.text: To applaud, hold the hands out and away from the body... with the palms facing each other. Bring palms together firmly and quickly and continue to breathe normally.
jokes.setup: Flight safety announcement format applied to talk show
jokes.punchline: Absurdly detailed instructions for basic human action of clapping
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy - treating the mundane as complex procedure. Strong originality and structure.

dialogue.line: To applaud, hold the hands out and away from the body... with the palms facing each other. Bring palms together firmly and quickly and continue to breathe normally.

jokes.text: In the event of a sudden loss of interest in one of tonight's guests... your seat cushion may be removed and used as a pillow.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about boring guests with practical solution
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy about the show itself within absurdist framework. Perfect economy and self-awareness.

dialogue.line: In the event of a sudden loss of interest in one of tonight's guests... your seat cushion may be removed and used as a pillow.

jokes.text: I think the only thing Karen forgot to add... is please do not stand until we've come to a complete halt... which I think we just have.
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about show's momentum stopping
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that extends the bit while being self-deprecating about the show's pacing

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I think the only thing Karen forgot to add... is please do not stand until we've come to a complete halt... which I think we just have.

jokes.text: the bad thing about earthquakes when they happen in Los Angeles... is it makes it more difficult to shoot straight.
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about gun violence/drugs being the real concern
jokes.explanation: Dark LA joke with good misdirection, though feels more like stand-up material

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: the bad thing about earthquakes when they happen in Los Angeles... is it makes it more difficult to shoot straight.

jokes.text: she started to yell, 'It's the big one.' And I said, 'Thank you very much.' And then she said, 'No, it's the quake.'
jokes.punchline: Sexual misunderstanding during natural disaster
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual misdirection - competent but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: she started to yell, 'It's the big one.' And I said, 'Thank you very much.' And then she said, 'No, it's the quake.'

jokes.text: After the show was over, they laughed for an hour... they had fun with us for an hour, then they got dressed and just left.
jokes.setup: Complaint about last night's audience behavior
jokes.punchline: Treating audience like one-night stand
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection comparing audience to sexual partner - well-constructed

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: After the show was over, they laughed for an hour... they had fun with us for an hour, then they got dressed and just left.

jokes.text: Please, stay. I like to cuddle afterwards. And, you never know, an hour later I may feel like putting on another show.
jokes.punchline: Double meaning of 'show' - performance vs sexual performance
jokes.explanation: Extends the metaphor competently but becomes more obvious

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Please, stay. I like to cuddle afterwards. And, you never know, an hour later I may feel like putting on another show.

jokes.text: Sleep well. Remember, we'll all be sleeping at the same time.
jokes.punchline: Pointlessly obvious observation delivered as profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry Sanders absurdism - stating the obvious as if meaningful. Great character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Sleep well. Remember, we'll all be sleeping at the same time.

jokes.text: My house? I'm in a lawsuit with the contractor... because the walls are bleeding like a miracle.
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions supernatural horror as legal matter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist throwaway - treating miraculous horror as mundane legal issue. Excellent economy.

dialogue.speaker: Dana
dialogue.line: My house? I'm in a lawsuit with the contractor... because the walls are bleeding like a miracle.

jokes.text: This is an office, not a club. David, I know this is total torture... but you gotta do your act for me so Larry can hear the content.
jokes.setup: Writer has to perform stand-up in office setting
jokes.punchline: Acknowledgment of how awkward and wrong this situation is
jokes.explanation: Good behind-the-scenes cringe about the mechanics of show business. Character-specific.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: This is an office, not a club. David, I know this is total torture... but you gotta do your act for me so Larry can hear the content.

jokes.text: Like anyone goes, 'Oh, he's good. I like his work.'
jokes.punchline: Absurd idea that passengers would recognize pilot like celebrity

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Like anyone goes, 'Oh, he's good. I like his work.'

jokes.text: And the flight attendant will come by and pull the curtain shut. 'Sorry.'
jokes.punchline: Apologetic dismissal of coach passengers as losers
jokes.explanation: Competent observational material about class anxiety - nothing special

dialogue.line: And the flight attendant will come by and pull the curtain shut. 'Sorry.'

jokes.text: You make that little face? That is so Spade, and Larry loves that.

jokes.explanation: Sharp industry satire about commodifying personality - good character work

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: You make that little face? That is so Spade, and Larry loves that.

jokes.text: You will not. You will not change a thing. That's what this show needs... penis, and plenty of it.
jokes.punchline: Producer's absurdly emphatic commitment to penis content
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation from censorship discussion to absurd manifesto. Great quotability.

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: You will not. You will not change a thing. That's what this show needs... penis, and plenty of it.

jokes.text: How many times you been on, Paula? Never. Exactly. So show Mr. Spade here a little respect.
jokes.punchline: Producer has never been on her own show
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about show business hierarchy and Larry's thoughtlessness

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: How many times you been on, Paula?

dialogue.speaker: Paula
dialogue.line: Never.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Exactly. So show Mr. Spade here a little respect.

jokes.text: You can't say 'penis.' Got it.

jokes.explanation: Simple callback showing comedian's powerlessness - economical cringe

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You can't say 'penis.' Got it.

jokes.text: The Hon... No, I've got a new Mustang. It's brand-new.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about car, David starts to mention Honda
jokes.punchline: Catches himself, lies about having cooler car
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing insecurity and class anxiety in real time

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: The Hon... No, I've got a new Mustang. It's brand-new.

jokes.text: I guess it's that time. Thanks for coming by. So... Long? So long. I'm gonna go drive the Mustang.
jokes.punchline: Multiple false endings and David's pathetic exit line
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - multiple attempts to end conversation, pathetic callback to lie

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I guess it's that time. Thanks for coming by.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So... Long? So long.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm gonna go drive the Mustang.

jokes.text: Nice kid.
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely inadequate assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan - massive understatement after cringe fest. Excellent economy and character.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Nice kid.

jokes.text: I am not allowed to talk about show business, Artie. Put on Leno, honey. Please?
jokes.setup: Larry claims he's not allowed to discuss business
jokes.punchline: Immediately demands to watch competitor's show
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Larry's self-imposed rule immediately violated by jealousy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I am not allowed to talk about show business, Artie. Put on Leno, honey. Please?

jokes.text: I thought he was doing our show tomorrow, Artie. What the fuck is this?
jokes.setup: Seeing Spade on Leno after booking him
jokes.punchline: Larry's explosion of betrayed rage
jokes.explanation: Natural character reaction establishing central conflict - functional plot moment

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: I thought he was doing our show tomorrow, Artie. What the fuck is this?

jokes.text: You don't do two talk shows back-to-back. It's an unspoken rule. Maybe it's so unspoken that he didn't know about it.
jokes.punchline: Jeannie's logical observation about unspoken rules
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay exposing the absurdity of unspoken rules - good logic and economy

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: You don't do two talk shows back-to-back. It's an unspoken rule. Maybe it's so unspoken that he didn't know about it.

jokes.text: Maybe it's so unspoken that he didn't know about it.
jokes.punchline: Points out logical flaw in unspoken rules
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that exposes the absurdity of 'unspoken rules.' Sharp and logical.

dialogue.line: Maybe it's so unspoken that he didn't know about it.

jokes.text: My feelings are hurt, honey, and by a man.
jokes.punchline: Unnecessary specification of gender as if that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry character moment - vulnerable but with bizarre emphasis on masculinity

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: My feelings are hurt, honey, and by a man.

jokes.punchline: Elaborate plan involving starving large actress to attack comedian

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Book Nell Carter on the show. We'll lock her in her dressing room for three days and don't feed her. Then throw David Spade in there with a piece of HoneyBaked ham down his pants.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: All those years in the merchant marine have served you well, Paula.

jokes.text: If I can't, I'm gonna dress up like Nell Carter and kick his ass myself.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: If I can't, I'm gonna dress up like Nell Carter and kick his ass myself.

dialogue.line: By the way, Lenny and Squiggy say hi.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: Sure. I saw Buddy Ebsen's head floating over my dresser. But he vanished. Slept like a dream.

jokes.text: I'm surprised you didn't smell Leno's cologne on the little rat bastard.

dialogue.speaker: Artie
dialogue.line: I'm surprised you didn't smell Leno's cologne on the little rat bastard.

dialogue.line: My pal Ray Combs hosts Family Feud, he's getting her on his show for me. Is that right?

dialogue.line: No, just her.

dialogue.speaker: Hank
dialogue.line: Sometimes when they have a family with just four people... they slap on a fifth person and make them a cousin or whatever. They do that? Hush-hush.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: Can't you hear my voice that I'm saying no to you? Please? I'm saying no. We're such good friends, and you can't hear that I'm saying no to you?
